tooltips.lpxKind: Custom LPX supports symmetrical buy/sell zones in a limited range. It's optimal for custom yield strategies, using NTZ from 0.20%-100%. Directional LPX is designed for execution-only setups—either buy or sell side only, no yield. It's used for strategic value extraction within a limited range.
tooltips.managedMode: Solo: Individual pool, you control all settings. Can be upgraded to Manager. Managed: NTZ, Max & Min Price settings can be changed by manager. Anyone can deposit and withdraw anytime. Immutable: Settings are locked forever. Anyone can deposit or withdraw anytime. No one can change parameters or manage.
tooltips.aprSinceInception: Since Inception APR is a live estimate of annualized return based on realized trades since launch and current market conditions. It includes realized trade profits and reinvested income, expressed in Anchor tokens. Because the metric is annualized using current conditions, actual future returns may differ. Periods of low or no trading activity may reduce the displayed APR.
tooltips.noTradeZone: No Trade Zone (NTZ) is the range where LPX is waiting for price volatility. NTZ is the price gap between the buy and sell zones.
tooltips.profit: Profit portion of NTZ you get as a gain.
tooltips.reinvested: Reinvested portion of profit coming back into LPX as deposit to increase working capital and compounding.
tooltips.managerReward: Manager Reward is the part of NTZ paid to the manager wallet for managing the LPX position. The incentive pays the manager only from profits, not your principal.
tooltips.fee: NTZ protocol fee taken from each buy or sell.
tooltips.maxSellPrice: Max Price sets the upper limit of your LPX sell zone. Once the token price reaches this point, LPX will be out of fund token. A lower max price means tighter range with larger volume per trade.
tooltips.sellPrice: Sell Price marks the start of the LPX fund token sell zone. Once the fund token price rises above this point, LPX begins selling. Lower sell price triggers earlier profit-taking, while a higher sell price delays selling but allows for more upside before LPX activates.
tooltips.buyPrice: Buy Price marks the start of the LPX buy zone with anchor token. When the token price drops below this point, LPX begins buying. A higher buy price means LPX enters sooner, while a lower buy price waits for deeper dips before activating.
tooltips.minBuyPrice: Min Price sets the lower boundary of your LPX buy zone. LPX will be out of anchor and stop buying if the token price drops to this level. A tighter min price increases buy impact but narrows the range. A wider range offers more coverage but reduces per-trade effect.
tooltips.fund: Fund Token is the name of asset in the pair that goes first. You deposit fund token into LPX to provide liquidity. This can be any supported token and determines which side (buy, sell, or both) your LPX position will operate on. It also defines what yield and exposure you receive. You get more fund as yield when reinvesting to compound.
tooltips.anchor: Anchor Token is the second asset in the LPX pair, opposite to your Fund Token. It defines the price reference for trades and determines what you buy with your fund. Anchor is the yield you claim from LPX.
tooltips.manager: Manager Wallet is a wallet with limited permissions to adjust LPX settings. It cannot withdraw funds or change tokens, ensuring your assets remain fully secure while allowing strategic management.
tooltips.myPosition: Combined value of your Fund and Anchor allocations, including reinvested beneficial interest. Share positions are shown in brackets. You keep exclusive cryptographic control and full redemption rights.
tooltips.yield: Private property and beneficial interests available for immediate claim. This variable yield is generated by LPX protocol mechanics—such as arbitrage and volatility capture.
tooltips.totalTvl: Total value of all assets committed across all active LPX positions on this interface.
tooltips.totalPoolVolume: Aggregate trading volume processed by all LPX pools in the selected time window.
tooltips.totalThroughput: Total notional value routed through LPX execution logic, including buy and sell trades across all active positions.
tooltips.impermanentLoss: The difference in value between holding tokens in LPX versus holding them outside the protocol (HODL). Negative values indicate LPX underperformed a static hold; positive values indicate outperformance.
tooltips.fees24h: Protocol and manager fees collected across all trades in the last 24 hours for this pool.
tooltips.makerYield7d: Yield generated from LPX acting as a maker in the market over the last 7 days, expressed in anchor tokens.
tooltips.arbitrageYield7d: Yield captured from price arbitrage opportunities over the last 7 days, expressed in anchor tokens.

activePools.label: Active Pools
activePools.subtitle: across 10 fund tokens
activePools.tooltip: Count of LPX positions currently open and accepting deposits.

liquidityProviders.label: Liquidity Providers
liquidityProviders.subtitle: 1,274 all-time
liquidityProviders.tooltip: Unique addresses currently providing liquidity across all active LPX pools. All-time figure includes addresses that have since exited.

avgApr30d.label: Avg 30D APR
avgApr30d.subtitle: Max 17.75% on HEX/HEX
avgApr30d.tooltip: Mean of the 30-day annualized percentage rate across all active LPX pools. Past performance does not guarantee future yield.

headerStatsNote: Three sparkline-and-delta cards from the mockup are replaced by computed aggregates from the live LPX API (SearchLPXs). The mockup's Total TVL / Total Pool Volume / Total Throughput do not appear on the real lpx.plusx.app interface and would have required price oracle integration to compute honestly. These three card metrics auto-refresh in v1.1 when the client switches to live API fetching.

disclaimer.lpxTermsOfUseExcerpt:
22. Technology and Market Risk

Use of LPX involves substantial risk. These risks include, without limitation:

Smart-contract bugs, exploits, failures, upgrade issues, or design flaws
Blockchain congestion, reorgs, failed transactions, halted execution, or fork events
Gas shortages, gas spikes, or insufficient gas provisioning
Wallet compromise, key loss, phishing, malware, approval misuse, or signing error
Oracle, routing, pricing, or data-feed issues
Volatility, slippage, thin liquidity, idle capital, missed trades, adverse market movement, failure to re-enter at preferred levels, and reduction of one token balance relative to another
Configuration error, including mis-set zones, token choices, or LPX mode selections
Dependency risk involving bridges, explorers, wallets, aggregators, and other External Resources

Each Member assumes such risks to the fullest extent permitted by the Covenant and applicable law.
disclaimer.upEcosystemRiskExcerpt: Currency Risk: The assets used in uP (Pulsechain or PLS) can change in value compared to the US dollar, which can be risky for those buying uP tokens or PLS. Market Risk: The value of uP's assets can go down at any time because of an open market (big wallets are welcome to sell at any time), which could lead to an increase or decrease in the worth of uP tokens. Operational User Risk: There's a risk of losing assets due to the user's inability to perform (for example burning tokens by mistake). To mitigate the risk, user centric design is implemented. Market Sentiment: Investor attitudes like fear, uncertainty, and doubt (FUD) can greatly affect the price and availability of an asset. Things like news, social media, and public opinion can quickly change how the market feels, causing unpredictable price changes.
disclaimer.websiteTermsIntro: Welcome to PlusX.app (the "Website"). This is a PulseChain community-driven website interface to run code. By accessing or using this Website, you agree to comply with and be bound by these Terms of Use. No Financial Advice: Any information on this site is for educational and entertainment purposes only. Always consult a qualified financial professional and use test wallets before making transactions. PlusX.app does not provide financial, investment, or trading advice.
